title: rubus ostryifolius
intro: Rubus ostryifolius, also known as the Japanese raspberry or Japanese wineberry, is a species of bramble in the rose family, native to Japan and Korea. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus ostryifolius include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as possessing potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, stems, and fruits are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
